source.description: Approved six-panel raster contact sheet cropped to reviewed JPG masters.
source.notes: One centered side-view sparrow stays consistent in every panel.

process_strategy: Branch, body, head, beak, wing, tail, feet, berries, and texture appear before the final only darkens keeper lines and shades.

final_elements.name: branch and berries
final_elements.description: A curved perch with a short twig and three round berries.

final_elements.name: sparrow body and head
final_elements.description: A plump side-view body and smaller rounded head centered directly above the branch.

final_elements.name: sparrow contour
final_elements.description: The clean outer back, belly, and tapered-tail silhouette drawn over the body construction.

final_elements.name: beak wing tail and feet
final_elements.description: A small triangular beak, round eye, layered wing, divided tail, and two feet gripping the branch.

final_elements.name: feather shading
final_elements.description: Short feather marks and gentle graphite tone on the already drawn wing, belly, branch, and berries.

frames.visible_job: Draw a gently curved branch, a short berry twig, three berry circles, and a light body axis.
Add one large body oval and one smaller overlapping head oval centered above the established branch.
Turn the construction into a rounded back, soft belly, and tapered tail that follows the same diagonal.
Draw the small beak, round eye, layered wing, two gripping feet, and tail divisions on the established bird.
Add short feather marks and light directional graphite shade to the established wing, belly, branch, and berries.

final_step.visible_job: Darken only the existing keeper contours and clarify the existing beak, eye, wing, feet, branch, berries, and graphite shading.

rejection_checks: No major element first appears in the final.
One pose crop and scale throughout.
